metadata.title: VIII
notes: H.R. 1968 is a continuing resolution (CR). Section 1101 continues FY2024 appropriations (Division E of P.L. 118-42, H.R. 4366) at the same rate and under the same terms and conditions. Sections 1801 and 1802 provide specific anomalies that override the default CR level for certain accounts.

Accounts not mentioned in Sec. 1801 or 1802 (OIG, Buildings & Facilities, Hazardous Substance Superfund, LUST, Inland Oil Spill, WIFIA) continue at their FY2024 enacted levels with FY2024 terms and conditions.

For accounts with anomalies (S&T, EPM, STAG), the CR sets a new total and selectively modifies FY2024 provisos via text substitutions. Unmodified provisos and sub-allocations carry forward at FY2024 levels.

The 'available_until' dates shown reflect the FY2024 text as carried forward. Under CR mechanics, dates referencing 'September 30, 2025' in FY2024 law would typically remain as stated (since FY2025 funds are available through that date). Multi-year and no-year funds retain their original availability.

accounts.name: Science and Technology

sub_items.name: Research: National Priorities
notes: FY2024 included $2,030,000 within this amount for Community Project Funding/CDS items. Sec. 1801(7) substitutes '$0' for '$2,030,000', zeroing out CDS earmarks.

sub_items.name: Unallocated/general
notes: Remainder of S&T not earmarked by provisos. Covers R&D activities including CERCLA research, personnel, travel, lab equipment, aircraft, and other operating expenses.
In addition to the direct appropriation, $30,343,000 is transferred in from the Hazardous Substance Superfund account for S&T activities.
Sec. 1801(7) anomaly: sets level at $756,073,000 (FY2024 was $758,103,000, a reduction of $2,030,000 matching the zeroed CDS earmarks).
FY2024 terms and conditions apply with the substitutions specified in Sec. 1801(7).

accounts.name: Environmental Programs and Management

sub_items.name: Main appropriation
Environmental Protection: National Priorities
notes: As specified in the FY2024 explanatory statement. Continues at FY2024 level.

sub_items.name: Geographic Programs
notes: As specified in the FY2024 explanatory statement. Continues at FY2024 level.
Includes Great Lakes Restoration Initiative and other geographic programs. Up to $368,000,000 of GLRI funds may be transferred to other Federal agencies per the FY2024 administrative provisions.

sub_items.name: Alaska Native contaminated lands grants
notes: Grants to Alaska Native entities to address contamination on lands conveyed under the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act. Up to $2,000,000 may be reserved for program administration.

sub_items.name: Unallocated/general
notes: Remainder of main EPM appropriation not earmarked by provisos. Covers personnel, travel, motor vehicles, aircraft, reprints, library memberships, brownfields admin costs, coal combustion residual permit program, and up to $40,000 for official reception and representation expenses.
FY2025 level is $17,000,000 above FY2024 ($3,178,028,000). The increase is absorbed in this unallocated remainder.
Sec. 1802(3) anomaly: sets level at $3,195,028,000 (FY2024 was $3,178,028,000, an increase of $17,000,000).

sub_items.name: TSCA Service Fee Fund activities
notes: Separate 'In addition' paragraph under the EPM heading in FY2024 law, continuing at FY2024 rate.

For activities under section 26(b)(1) of TSCA (15 U.S.C. 2625(b)(1)). $9,000,000 appropriated from general fund, offset by TSCA service fee receipts deposited as discretionary offsetting receipts, resulting in net $0 from general fund.
Fees in excess of $9,000,000 are also retained in the TSCA Service Fee Fund.
The Chemical Risk Review and Reduction program project must be allocated not less than the FY2014 appropriation level (excluding fees).
Account total combines the main appropriation ($3,195,028,000 per Sec. 1802(3) anomaly) and the TSCA 'In addition' paragraph ($9,000,000 continuing at FY2024 rate).
FY2024 terms and conditions apply. No proviso modifications specified for EPM in the CR.

accounts.name: Office of Inspector General
notes: Continues at FY2024 level under Sec. 1101 (no anomaly).
Subject to terms, conditions, and requirements specified under this heading in Senate Report 118-83.
In addition to the direct appropriation, $11,328,000 is transferred in from the Hazardous Substance Superfund account.

accounts.name: Buildings and Facilities
notes: Continues at FY2024 level under Sec. 1101 (no anomaly).
For construction, repair, improvement, extension, alteration, and purchase of fixed equipment or facilities.

accounts.name: Hazardous Substance Superfund

sub_items.name: Transfer to Office of Inspector General
notes: Paid to the OIG appropriation.

sub_items.name: Transfer to Science and Technology
notes: Paid to the S&T appropriation.

sub_items.name: Unallocated/general (CERCLA response activities)
notes: Remainder for carrying out CERCLA, including sections 111(c)(3), (c)(5), (c)(6), and (e)(4). Funds may be allocated to other Federal agencies per CERCLA section 111(a).
Continues at FY2024 level under Sec. 1101 (no anomaly).

Funded from the Hazardous Substance Superfund trust fund (sums available in the Trust Fund on September 30, 2023, not otherwise appropriated, per SARA section 517(a)) and up to $537,700,000 as a payment from general revenues per SARA section 517(b).
Includes hire, maintenance, and operation of aircraft.

accounts.name: Leaking Underground Storage Tank Trust Fund Program

sub_items.name: Leaking underground storage tank cleanup (SWDA section 9003(h))
Other SWDA provisions per IRC section 9508(c)
notes: For carrying out provisions of the Solid Waste Disposal Act specified in IRC section 9508(c) other than section 9003(h).
Administrator authorized to use these funds to implement SWDA section 9013 for financial assistance to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Continues at FY2024 level under Sec. 1101 (no anomaly).

accounts.name: Inland Oil Spill Programs
notes: Continues at FY2024 level under Sec. 1101 (no anomaly).
Derived from the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund.
For EPA responsibilities under the Oil Pollution Act of 1990, including aircraft hire, maintenance, and operation.

accounts.name: State and Tribal Assistance Grants

sub_items.name: Clean Water State Revolving Fund capitalization grants
sub_items.statutory_reference: Federal Water Pollution Control Act, title VI
notes: FY2024 included $787,652,267 within this amount for CDS/Community Project Funding construction grants. Sec. 1801(8) substitutes '$0' for '$787,652,267', zeroing out the CDS earmark. The full $1,638,861,000 is now available for standard SRF capitalization.
FY2024's second proviso under paragraph (1) (allowing up to $13,300,000 for CDS admin from combined SRF funds) shall not apply per Sec. 1801(8).
FY2024 proviso requiring 10% green infrastructure/environmentally innovative activities continues (for eligible projects consistent with State Intended Use Plans).
10% of capitalization grants must be used for additional subsidy (forgiveness, negative interest, or grants).
Up to $1,500,000 may be used for Clean Watersheds Needs Survey.
Up to 2% (or $30,000,000, whichever greater) may be reserved for grants to tribes under FWPCA section 518(c).
Up to 1.5% of aggregate CW SRF (less tribal set-aside) may be reserved for territories under FWPCA title II.

sub_items.name: Drinking Water State Revolving Fund capitalization grants
notes: FY2024 included $631,659,905 within this amount for CDS/Community Project Funding construction grants. Sec. 1801(8) substitutes '$0' for '$631,659,905', zeroing out the CDS earmark. The full $1,126,101,000 is now available for standard SRF capitalization.
FY2024 proviso permitting (not requiring) green infrastructure use at State discretion continues.
14% of capitalization grants must be used for additional subsidy (forgiveness, negative interest, or grants).
Up to 2% (or $20,000,000, whichever greater) may be reserved for grants to tribes under SDWA section 1452(i).
Up to 1.5% may be reserved for territories under SDWA section 1452(j).
Up to $12,000,000 shall be reserved for unregulated contaminant monitoring costs under SDWA section 1445(a)(2)(C).

sub_items.name: US-Mexico Border water/wastewater infrastructure
notes: Architectural, engineering, planning, design, construction for high priority water/wastewater facilities in the US-Mexico border area. Colonia development restrictions apply.

sub_items.name: Alaska rural and Native village water/wastewater
notes: Grants to State of Alaska. 25% State match required. Max 5% for admin/overhead. Awards must be consistent with statewide priority list; at least 25% for regional hub communities.

sub_items.name: Brownfields (CERCLA section 104(k))
notes: Grants, interagency agreements, and program support costs. At least 10% for persistent poverty counties.

sub_items.name: Diesel emissions reduction grants (Energy Policy Act title VII, subtitle G)
sub_items.statutory_reference: Energy Policy Act of 2005, title VII, subtitle G

sub_items.name: Targeted airshed grants
notes: In accordance with terms and conditions in the FY2024 explanatory statement.

sub_items.name: Small and disadvantaged community drinking water assistance (SDWA section 1459A(a)-(j))
sub_items.statutory_reference: Safe Drinking Water Act, section 1459A(a)-(j) (42 U.S.C. 300j-19a)
notes: May also be used by States to assist private well owners for contaminant-related activities, and to support underserved communities.

sub_items.name: Voluntary school and child care lead testing (SDWA section 1464(d))
sub_items.statutory_reference: Safe Drinking Water Act, section 1464(d) (42 U.S.C. 300j-24(d))

sub_items.name: Lead service line replacement grants (SDWA section 1459B)
sub_items.statutory_reference: Safe Drinking Water Act, section 1459B (42 U.S.C. 300j-19b)

sub_items.name: Operational sustainability grants (SDWA section 1459A(l))
sub_items.statutory_reference: Safe Drinking Water Act, section 1459A(l) (42 U.S.C. 300j-19a(l))

sub_items.name: Sewer overflow and stormwater reuse grants (FWPCA section 104(b)(8))
sub_items.statutory_reference: Federal Water Pollution Control Act, section 104(b)(8) (33 U.S.C. 1254(b)(8))

sub_items.name: Decentralized wastewater/alternative water source grants (FWPCA section 221)
sub_items.statutory_reference: Federal Water Pollution Control Act, section 221 (33 U.S.C. 1301)

sub_items.name: Water infrastructure workforce development (AWIA 2018 section 4304(b))
sub_items.statutory_reference: America's Water Infrastructure Act of 2018, section 4304(b) (P.L. 115-270)

sub_items.name: Post-consumer materials municipal grants (Save Our Seas 2.0 Act section 302(a))
sub_items.statutory_reference: Save Our Seas 2.0 Act, section 302(a) (33 U.S.C. 4282(a))
notes: Not more than 2% for administrative costs. May also provide grants to intertribal consortia, former Indian reservations in Oklahoma, and Alaska Native Villages.

sub_items.name: Wildfire smoke preparedness grants (Clean Air Act section 103(b)(3))
notes: In accordance with FY2024 explanatory statement terms. Not more than 3% for administrative costs.

sub_items.name: Community Project Funding/CDS items (STAG line)
notes: FY2024 level was $38,693,000 for CDS items specified in the FY2024 explanatory statement. Sec. 1801(8) substitutes '$0' for '$38,693,000', completely eliminating this line item. This accounts for the entire $38,693,000 reduction from the FY2024 STAG total.

sub_items.name: Assistance for small and underserved communities (SDWA section 1459F)
sub_items.statutory_reference: Safe Drinking Water Act, section 1459F (42 U.S.C. 300j-19g)

sub_items.name: Water systems technical assistance (AWIA 2018 section 2001)
sub_items.statutory_reference: America's Water Infrastructure Act of 2018, section 2001 (P.L. 115-270, 42 U.S.C. 300j-3c note)
notes: Administrator may award grants and contracts to tribes, intertribal consortia, public/private agencies, institutions, organizations, and individuals, and enter into interagency agreements.

sub_items.name: Stormwater infrastructure grants (IIJA section 50217(b))
sub_items.statutory_reference: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, section 50217(b) (33 U.S.C. 1302f(b); P.L. 117-58)

sub_items.name: Enhanced aquifer use and recharge grants (FWPCA section 124)
sub_items.statutory_reference: Federal Water Pollution Control Act, section 124 (33 U.S.C. 1276)

sub_items.name: Categorical/multi-media grants to States and tribes
notes: Grants to States, federally recognized tribes, interstate agencies, tribal consortia, and air pollution control agencies for pollution prevention, control, and abatement.

Of which: $46,250,000 for CERCLA section 128 (State response programs); $9,500,000 for Environmental Information Exchange Network grants; $1,475,000 for SWDA section 2007(f)(2) State grants (in addition to LUST funds); $18,512,000 of section 106 (FWPCA) grants for State water resources surveys and monitoring enhancements.
Also includes grants under P.L. 104-134, Clean Air Act section 103 (PM monitoring), and SWDA section 2301 (coal combustion residuals).

sub_items.name: Prior-year rescission offset
notes: FY2024 permanently rescinded $1,500,000 of unobligated balances from pre-FY2012 'special project grants'/'special needs infrastructure grants'. The enumerated sub-items sum to $1,500,000 more than the account total in both FY2024 and FY2025. This reconciliation item reflects the netting of the rescission against new budget authority in the account total as scored.

Under a CR, this one-time rescission from FY2024 does not repeat, but the FY2025 total ($4,380,245,000 = FY2024 $4,418,938,000 minus zeroed item 16 $38,693,000) inherits the same structural discrepancy.
Sec. 1801(8) anomaly: sets level at $4,380,245,000 (FY2024 was $4,418,938,000, a reduction of $38,693,000 matching the zeroed CDS line item).

Three CDS/earmark provisions zeroed: $787,652,267 within CW SRF, $631,659,905 within DW SRF, and $38,693,000 standalone CDS grants. The SRF earmark zeroing does not change the SRF totals — it removes the earmark restrictions, freeing funds for general SRF capitalization.
The second proviso under paragraph (1) of the FY2024 STAG heading (allowing up to $13,300,000 for CDS admin from combined SRF funds) is explicitly made inapplicable per Sec. 1801(8).
FY2024 CDS procurement flexibility proviso continues for prior-year CDS grants but is moot for FY2025 since all FY2025 CDS amounts are zeroed.

accounts.name: Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Program Account

sub_items.name: Cost of direct loans and guaranteed loans
notes: Costs as defined in section 502 of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974.
Subsidizes gross obligations for principal of direct loans (including capitalized interest) and total loan principal of guaranteed loans not to exceed $12,500,000,000.
Of which $5,000,000 solely for projects described in WIFIA section 5026(9) (State infrastructure financing authorities, per WIFIA section 5033(e)).
Loans must comply with criteria published in 85 FR 39189 (June 30, 2020). Administrator and OMB Director must certify compliance in advance.
Fees collected under WIFIA sections 5029 and 5030 are also deposited in this account (in addition to appropriated amounts).

sub_items.name: Administrative expenses
notes: Separate 'In addition' paragraph for admin expenses, notwithstanding WIFIA section 5033.
Continues at FY2024 level under Sec. 1101 (no anomaly).
Total combines the loan subsidy appropriation ($64,634,000, available until expended) and the administrative expenses appropriation ($7,640,000, available until September 30, 2025).
